title: The Pythagorean Theorem and Its Converse

flashcards.definition: a² + b² = c²; relates the legs and hypotenuse of a right triangle.
The longest side of a right triangle, opposite the right angle.
Three positive integers a, b, c where a² + b² = c² (e.g., 3-4-5, 5-12-13).
If a² + b² = c², the triangle is right. If a² + b² > c², acute. If <, obtuse.
Derived from the Pythagorean Theorem applied to coordinate geometry.
